Title: Seed-coat protective neolignans are produced by the dirigent protein AtDP1 and the laccase AtLAC5 in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutants (atdp1-1, atdp1-2); genomic complementation; seed metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS/QTOF-MS; RT-PCR/RT-qPCR; promoter-YFP and promoter-GUS localization; tetrazolium permeability assay
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: SC/FC-conjugated neolignans, especially erythro-SC(4-O-8)G and erythro-FC(4-O-8)G
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed coat protection; reduced permeability to xenobiotic tetrazolium salts; likely chemical defense
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing seed coat, especially outer integument 1 cells
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AtDP1 abolishes or strongly reduces seed neolignans and increases seed coat permeability
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. atdp1 mutants lack the seed-specific neolignans SC(4-O-8)G and FC(4-O-8)G and show reduced FC(5-8)G-type neolignans. 2. Genomic complementation restores the metabolic defect, establishing AtDP1 as essential for seed neolignan biosynthesis. 3. AtDP1 is specifically expressed in developing seed outer integument 1 cells, and its loss increases seed coat permeability without major changes in PA or lignin in atdp1.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly in seeds at developmental stages 9-11
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-specific; strongest in developing seeds, outer integument 1 cells
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secretory pathway-associated/apoplastic predicted; YFP signal in outer integument 1 cells
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native AtDP1 promoter, 720 bp and genomic promoter fragments (0.72 kb and 1.8 kb)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds; seed developmental stages 9-11; tetrazolium penetration assay
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased seed coat permeability when disrupted
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtDP1 is a dirigent protein, not an enzyme or transcription factor. It directs regio- and stereoselective radical coupling and is therefore classified as a Common Gene rather than Pathway or Regulation gene.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression analysis; T-DNA knockout mutants (lac5-2, lac5-3); RT-PCR; seed metabolite profiling by UPLC-QTOF-MS; promoter-GUS assays; lignin thioacidolysis; tetrazolium permeability assay
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: FC-conjugated neolignans, including erythro-FC(4-O-8)G, FC(5-8)G, and FC(5-8)G 4-O-Hex
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed coat protection; likely contributes with lignin to barrier properties
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AtLAC5 strongly reduces FC-conjugated neolignans and increases seed coat permeability; also lowers guaiacyl lignin monomers
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lac5 mutants show reductions of FC-conjugated neolignans to levels comparable to atdp1. 2. AtLAC5 is not required for most SC-conjugated neolignan accumulation, suggesting substrate preference for guaiacyl-type substrates. 3. lac5 mutants also show reduced guaiacyl lignin monomers, indicating dual roles in FC-neolignan and G-lignin formation in seeds.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in seeds, especially developmental stages 9-11; transcriptome suggests seed coat enrichment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-enriched; likely outer integument-associated; GUS strongest in replum, septum, and funiculus, with unclear seed staining
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secretory pathway-associated predicted via N-terminal signal peptide
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native AtLAC5 promoters, 4,140 bp and 2,000 bp promoter-GUS constructs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher seed coat permeability; reduced guaiacyl-type lignin in mutants
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtLAC5 acts as an oxidase rather than a classic biosynthetic transferase/synthase, and also contributes to lignin formation; classified here as Common Gene per task schema for non-pathway catalytic support proteins outside primary enzyme-gene focus.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutants (sng2-2, sng2-3, sng2-4); metabolite profiling of neolignans and choline derivatives
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides SC and FC precursor pool for seed neolignan biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SCT/SCPL19 abolishes SC and FC and correspondingly eliminates SC/FC-conjugated neolignans
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sng2 mutants lack SC and FC as well as SC/FC-conjugated neolignans. 2. Genetic evidence supports that SC and FC are direct precursors for neolignan biosynthesis. 3. SCT/SCPL19 likely functions in planta as both sinapoylglucose:choline sinapoyltransferase and feruloylglucose:choline feruloyltransferase.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted secretory/vacuolar pathway based on SCPL family discussion
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although previously known, SCT/SCPL19 is experimentally used here as a core pathway-enabling gene for precursor formation to validate the neolignan route.
